event: mass extending up to the umbilicus
pelvic MRI showed mass measuring 18 × 17 × 12 cm
mass located on the left ovary
mass extending up to the umbilicus
no malodorous and greyish vaginal secretions
surgical pelvic exploration revealed 30-centimeter mass of left broad ligament
total hysterectomy with adnexectomy and removal of mass performed
contrast-enhanced computed tomography scan showed bilobed air and fluid collection suggesting abscess in retroperitoneum and extending along left psoas muscle and Douglas pouch
did not respond to intravenous volume replacement with 2 L of Ringer Lactate
vasopressor therapy (continuous infusion of noradrenaline) started
transferred to operating room for emergency revision surgery
peritoneal cavity exploration revealed non-purulent serosanguinous peritoneal fluid
no surgical wound explaining infection found
antibacterial treatment with piperacillin/tazobactam and gentamicin administered
patient extubated in postoperative recovery room without complication
still needed hemodynamic support with noradrenaline
microbiological analysis of peritoneal fluid identified Gardnerella vaginalis
Atopobium vaginae identified in peritoneal fluid
antibacterial susceptibility testing of Gardnerella vaginalis showed resistance to metronidazole and ciprofloxacin
antibacterial susceptibility testing of Gardnerella vaginalis showed susceptibility to penicillin G, amoxicillin/clavulanate, cefotaxime, clindamycin and vancomycin
antibacterial susceptibility testing of Atopobium vaginae showed susceptibility to all tested antibacterials including metronidazole
diagnosis: early postoperative peritonitis-induced septic shock caused by Gardnerella vaginalis and Atopobium vaginae
patient discharged from intensive care unit
